channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Why you should care about 2 power companies merging. Hint: affordability
item.description: NextEra Energy plans to acquire Dominion Energy to create the largest electricity producer in the United States.
item.content:encoded:
NextEra Energy plans to acquire Dominion Energy to create the largest electricity producer in the United States.

(Image credit: Marco Bello)
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha

item.title: Tuesday is a big primary day. Here's what you need to know
item.description: Six states are holding primaries May 19 that could help to decide the balance of power in Congress and in key state governments.
item.content:encoded:
Six states are holding primaries May 19 that could help to decide the balance of power in Congress and in key state governments.

(Image credit: Megan Varner)
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler

item.title: Is Kennedy heir Jack Schlossberg ready to lead?
item.description: NPR's Juana Summers speaks with Jack Schlossberg, Democratic candidate for New York's 12th Congressional District and the grandson of President John F. Kennedy.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Juana Summers speaks with Jack Schlossberg, Democratic candidate for New York's 12th Congressional District and the grandson of President John F. Kennedy.
item.dc:creator: Mia Venkat

item.title: This Ebola outbreak raises questions about when it all began — and the U.S. response
item.description: The sheer number of cases and deaths are a sign that the outbreak might have been smoldering before the virus was identified.
item.content:encoded:
The sheer number of cases and deaths are a sign that the outbreak might have been smoldering before the virus was identified.

(Image credit: Badru Katumba/AFP)
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Lambert

item.title: Jury dismisses all claims in Elon Musk's lawsuit against OpenAI CEO Sam Altman
item.description: Musk had sought to oust Altman from his leadership position over claims that he and others breached their duty to OpenAI's original nonprofit mission and unjustly enriched themselves.
item.content:encoded:
Musk had sought to oust Altman from his leadership position over claims that he and others breached their duty to OpenAI's original nonprofit mission and unjustly enriched themselves.

(Image credit: Godofredo A. Vásquez)
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch

item.title: NPR trims jobs in newsroom overhaul as it confronts era without public funding
item.description: NPR is offering buyouts to journalists as it overhauls its newsroom, with the threat of layoffs to follow. Two recent gifts totaling $113 million are primarily dedicated to NPR's tech infrastructure.
item.content:encoded:
NPR is offering buyouts to journalists as it overhauls its newsroom, with the threat of layoffs to follow. Two recent gifts totaling $113 million are primarily dedicated to NPR's tech infrastructure.

(Image credit: Bloomberg via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik

item.title: Son of radicals, Zayd Ayers Dohrn details a childhood underground and on the run
item.description: Dohrn's parents, Bill Ayers and Bernardine Dohrn, helped found the the Weather Underground. "I knew that the FBI was chasing us," he says. His memoir is Dangerous, Dirty, Violent, and Young.
item.content:encoded:
Dohrn's parents, Bill Ayers and Bernardine Dohrn, helped found the the Weather Underground. "I knew that the FBI was chasing us," he says. His memoir is Dangerous, Dirty, Violent, and Young.

(Image credit: David Handschuh)
item.dc:creator: Terry Gross

item.title: The Supreme Court avoids taking up a fight over Voting Rights Act enforcement for now
item.description: After recently weakening the Voting Rights Act, the Supreme Court avoided for now taking up a legal question that may severely limit enforcement of the law's remaining protections for minority voters.
item.content:encoded:
After recently weakening the Voting Rights Act, the Supreme Court avoided for now taking up a legal question that may severely limit enforcement of the law's remaining protections for minority voters.

(Image credit: Jemal Countess)
item.dc:creator: Hansi Lo Wang

item.title: Judge dismisses Trump's IRS lawsuit, paving the way for a settlement
item.description: The president sued the IRS and the Treasury Department in January, demanding $10 billion over the leak of his tax returns years ago.
item.content:encoded:
The president sued the IRS and the Treasury Department in January, demanding $10 billion over the leak of his tax returns years ago.

(Image credit: Tasos Katopodis)
item.dc:creator: Carrie Johnson

item.title: Sen. Bill Cassidy loses primary. And, WHO declares Ebola outbreak a global emergency
item.description: Sen. Bill Cassidy, who voted to convict Trump, lost the Republican primary in Louisiana. And, the WHO has declared a global health emergency over a new Ebola outbreak.
item.content:encoded:
Sen. Bill Cassidy, who voted to convict Trump, lost the Republican primary in Louisiana. And, the WHO has declared a global health emergency over a new Ebola outbreak.

(Image credit: Tyler Kaufman)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton
